review_summary: Textual grounding is sufficient for concept-focused questions, and no separate video clip is needed.

question_bank.question: Why is mapping every unseen word to a single UNK token a weak strategy for modern NLP models?
choices: It erases information that may be recoverable from the word's characters or morphemes.
It makes common words too frequent during training.
It prevents the model from using punctuation as tokens.
It forces the model to learn a separate vector for every sentence.
question_bank.explanation: A novel word often contains meaningful parts, such as a stem, affix, typo pattern, or morphology. Collapsing all unseen strings into one UNK token makes very different words indistinguishable.

question_bank.question: In learned subword tokenization, what is the main purpose of merging frequent adjacent character sequences?
choices: To build larger reusable units so common words stay compact while rare words remain decomposable.
To remove all punctuation before the model sees the text.
To guarantee every word becomes exactly one token.
To replace all morphology with a language-specific grammar table.
question_bank.explanation: Starting from characters gives coverage for almost any string. Merging frequent pairs creates useful larger units, reducing sequence length for common patterns while preserving the ability to represent unseen forms.
